flags.rewound_from_stage0_5_pivot: Case 6b (unsalvageable conjecture at stage_0.5 reject) + regression pattern (a) the headline Lemma 1 decomposition assumes the exposure-fiber/orbit equality the theorem is supposed to diagnose, and the reviewer explicitly states the kernel is non-flagship with no local repair — re-deriving on the same .tex would burn budget against a structural novelty-floor failure, not a fixable derivation slip.

proposed_from.topic: Sharp non-equivalence and strict-extension theorem between three leading interference / network spillover identification estimators that handle SUTVA violation under approximate neighborhood interference: the Approximate Neighborhood Interference (ANI) estimator of Leung (2022, Econometrica) using exposure-mapping projection at fixed neighborhood radius r, the network-cluster-robust estimator of Leung (2023, Econometrica) with HAC-style cluster-bandwidth selection, and the randomization-test estimator of Basse, Ding, Feller, Toulis (2024, Econometrica) using exact randomization-based inference under partial interference. All three target the direct + spillover average treatment effect under bounded-degree network and exposure-mapping additivity. The flagship question (axis b: non-equivalence frontier between three named published network estimators): characterize the sharp boundary in (network density, exposure-radius, randomization-design) parameter space at which the three estimators have asymptotically equivalent identifying functionals versus strictly different limit functionals, as a function of (i) the network degree distribution rho, (ii) the exposure-misspecification rate epsilon_M, and (iii) the cluster-bandwidth scaling b_n. The kernel claim is a closed-form spectral non-equivalence theorem: ANI, network-CR, and randomization-test estimators identify the same direct + spillover effect and achieve identical asymptotic variance if and only if a degree-radius condition mu_max(rho, epsilon_M) <= mu_star, where mu_star is a sharp threshold equal to the smallest singular value of an explicit observable kernel built from rho, epsilon_M, and b_n; when mu_max > mu_star, the three diverge — ANI biased proportional to exposure-misspecification, network-CR consistent but inefficient by bandwidth, randomization-test achieves the design-based efficiency bound. mu_star is a NEW mathematical object, NOT existence/absence from prior. Recovers Leung 2022 ANI consistency under correctly-specified exposure (epsilon_M=0) and provides the first sharp triple-non-equivalence boundary for ANI / network-CR / randomization-test estimators in closed form.
proposed_from.novelty_justification: {"repo_axis":"Closest in-catalogue prior art remains doc/API.md Section 1 Q1 residual-score geometry and the banked pid_network_exposure_v1 proposal. Q1 is algebraic panel residual geometry rather than network-interference estimator-map comparison; pid_network_exposure_v1 proves sharp finite graph-completion bounds but explicitly does not claim statistical inference, asymptotic coverage, or estimator efficiency. The revised finite C_n(eta) cone keeps this proposal on the estimator-equivalence frontier rather than relabelling the partial-ID catalogue entry.","published_axis":"Closest published papers are Leung2022ANI, Leung2023NCRI, BasseDingFellerToulis2024, GaoDing2025Network, PuelzBasseFellerToulis2022, WeinsteinNevo2026, and Savje2024MisspecifiedExposure. They give validity, randomization, regression/HAC, or misspecification results for individual objects; the epsilon-gap is the joint observable cone program Delta_eta and common-null cell N_n(eta) giving an iff frontier for equality of ANI point scores, network-cluster covariance, and design-orbit randomization covariance."}
seed_list: Prior work Leung2022ANI would predict fixed-radius ANI exposure IPW plus network HAC is the natural limiting object under approximate neighborhood interference. Our seed conjectures that ANI, network-cluster-robust, and randomization-test functionals agree only below a spectral compatibility threshold, because omitted high-leverage spillover tails can be invisible to radius-r exposure cells but visible to cluster or design-orbit scores.

Prior work Savje2024MisspecifiedExposure would predict that misspecified exposures can still define estimable contrasts. Our seed conjectures that small epsilon_M misspecification concentrated on high-degree neighborhoods creates a sharp rho-indexed bias frontier, because estimability of the misspecified contrast does not imply causal equivalence to the direct-plus-spillover target.

Prior work Leung2023NCRI would predict that low-conductance spectral clusters can make cluster-robust inference valid for network dependence. Our seed conjectures that network-cluster robustness and network HAC are incomparable across heavy-tailed degree sequences, because graph balls and spectral cuts can put the same covariance mass in different operator blocks.

Prior work BasseDingFellerToulis2024 would predict exact randomization inference under design-compatible exposure permutations. Our seed conjectures that cross-cluster ANI leakage makes the randomization null and the ANI exposure contrast non-equivalent, because the design orbit can be sharp while the causal exposure tail is not orbit-measurable.

Prior work AronowSamii2017Exposure would imply that a known exposure mapping operationalizes arbitrary interference for exposure-specific causal means. Our seed conjectures that exposure means and design-orbit means separate when the exposure propensity projection is not orbit-invariant, because feasible assignments with the same exposure label can lie in different randomization orbits.

Prior work KojevnikovMarmerSong2021 would predict HAC consistency from dependence decay balanced against network denseness. Our seed conjectures that low-conductance cluster robustness can be valid where HAC fails, because bandwidth balls over-cover bridge edges that spectral partitions cut away.

Prior work HudgensHalloran2008 would predict that partial-interference clusters give clean direct and indirect effects. Our seed conjectures that ANI with vanishing but nonzero cross-cluster leakage is a strict extension rather than a perturbation, because the partial-interference null can remain exact for a narrower orbit while the direct-plus-spillover estimand changes.

Prior proposal pid_network_exposure_v1 §11 says it does not claim statistical inference, asymptotic coverage, or an efficient estimator. Our seed conjectures that the shared-completion exposure gap induces a second-order variance frontier, because graph-completion compatibility changes the score covariance as well as the identified set.

Prior failed proposal panel_honest_did_phase_v1 review asked to identify exactly which estimator or diagnostic each result lacks. Our seed conjectures that named network estimator maps have a quotient-row-space phase transition, because ANI, cluster, and randomization scores are different projections of the same spillover response surface.

Prior work BasseFellerToulis2019 would predict that conditioning mechanisms give valid randomization tests under interference. Our seed conjectures that conditioning loses direct-plus-spillover identification unless the orbit sigma-field refines the exposure sigma-field, because validity of a sharp null is weaker than equality of exposure-effect functionals.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work Leung2022ANI would predict fixed-radius ANI exposure IPW plus network HAC is the natural limiting object under approximate neighborhood interference. Our seed conjectures that ANI, network-cluster-robust, and randomization-test functionals agree only below a spectral compatibility threshold, because omitted high-leverage spillover tails can be invisible to radius-r exposure cells but visible to cluster or design-orbit scores.

anchor_paper.author: Michael P. Leung
anchor_paper.claim: ANI permits nonzero effects beyond a fixed neighborhood radius and studies IPW exposure estimators with network HAC inference, but does not compare them sharply to cluster-robust or permutation-test targets.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Boundary/regime-shift pattern, not blocked: the extension exposes a strict equality/non-equivalence frontier among three named published workflows rather than merely weakening ANI assumptions.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from Leung2022ANI because network HAC already supplies the asymptotic variance for ANI estimators.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: Leung2022ANI does not compare the ANI score to Leung2023NCRI cluster partitions or BasseDingFellerToulis2024 design-orbit randomization scores.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work Savje2024MisspecifiedExposure would predict that misspecified exposures can still define estimable contrasts. Our seed conjectures that small epsilon_M misspecification concentrated on high-degree neighborhoods creates a sharp rho-indexed bias frontier, because estimability of the misspecified contrast does not imply causal equivalence to the direct-plus-spillover target.

anchor_paper.author: Fredrik Savje
anchor_paper.claim: Misspecified exposure mappings can define estimable contrasts, while separating estimability from the assumption that the mapping captures the full causal structure.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Misspecification-bound pattern, not blocked: the proposed phenomenon is concentration of small error on high-degree leverage points causing a phase change, not simply a weaker exposure assumption.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from Savje2024MisspecifiedExposure because that paper already studies misspecified exposure mappings.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: Savje separates estimability from causal interpretation but does not give rho-indexed sharp bias frontiers for ANI direct-plus-spillover targets.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work Leung2023NCRI would predict that low-conductance spectral clusters can make cluster-robust inference valid for network dependence. Our seed conjectures that network-cluster robustness and network HAC are incomparable across heavy-tailed degree sequences, because graph balls and spectral cuts can put the same covariance mass in different operator blocks.

anchor_paper.author: Michael P. Leung
anchor_paper.claim: Network cluster-robust inference is characterized by low-conductance clusters and spectral clustering, not by exposure-radius correctness.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Counterexample/incomparability pattern, not blocked: the refuted comparison is between named published HAC and cluster-robust workflows, with open graph-sequence classes rather than a hand-tuned instance.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from Leung2023NCRI because simulations already show cluster-robust methods can beat HAC when clusters exist.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: simulations are not a necessary-and-sufficient inclusion or incomparability theorem for covariance operators indexed by rho and b_n.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work BasseDingFellerToulis2024 would predict exact randomization inference under design-compatible exposure permutations. Our seed conjectures that cross-cluster ANI leakage makes the randomization null and the ANI exposure contrast non-equivalent, because the design orbit can be sharp while the causal exposure tail is not orbit-measurable.

anchor_paper.author: Guillaume Basse, Peng Ding, Avi Feller, Panos Toulis
anchor_paper.claim: Permutation tests for group-formation peer effects are exact for design-compatible exposure permutations, but their framework is not a sharp equivalence theorem for ANI tails.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Strict-extension pattern, not blocked: the extension changes the identified null/estimand relation when cross-cluster leakage is present, rather than applying randomization tests to a new setting.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from BasseDingFellerToulis2024 because invalid permutations already show design incompatibility.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: their invalid-permutation examples concern finite design validity, not equality with ANI exposure functionals under approximate cross-cluster spillovers.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work AronowSamii2017Exposure would imply that a known exposure mapping operationalizes arbitrary interference for exposure-specific causal means. Our seed conjectures that exposure means and design-orbit means separate when the exposure propensity projection is not orbit-invariant, because feasible assignments with the same exposure label can lie in different randomization orbits.

anchor_paper.author: Peter M. Aronow, Cyrus Samii
anchor_paper.claim: Exposure mappings integrate assignment designs, exposure mappings, and estimands for average causal effects under general interference.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: n/a - orthogonal to blocklist: the mechanism is non-invariance between exposure labels and randomization orbits, not a generic assumption weakening.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from AronowSamii2017Exposure because exposure propensities already account for the assignment design.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: exposure propensity identification need not imply equality with orbit-restricted randomization functionals.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work KojevnikovMarmerSong2021 would predict HAC consistency from dependence decay balanced against network denseness. Our seed conjectures that low-conductance cluster robustness can be valid where HAC fails, because bandwidth balls over-cover bridge edges that spectral partitions cut away.

anchor_paper.author: Denis Kojevnikov, Vadim Marmer, Kyungchul Song
anchor_paper.claim: Network HAC consistency depends on the tradeoff between dependence decay and network denseness.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Counterexample/incomparability pattern, not blocked: the target is a named network HAC condition versus a named cluster-robust condition over nondegenerate graph sequences.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from network HAC theory because bandwidth failure is just a standard denseness violation.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: the proposed gap compares graph-distance bandwidth balls to spectral low-conductance partitions, which network HAC theory alone does not order.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work HudgensHalloran2008 would predict that partial-interference clusters give clean direct and indirect effects. Our seed conjectures that ANI with vanishing but nonzero cross-cluster leakage is a strict extension rather than a perturbation, because the partial-interference null can remain exact for a narrower orbit while the direct-plus-spillover estimand changes.

anchor_paper.author: Michael G. Hudgens, M. Elizabeth Halloran
anchor_paper.venue: Journal of the American Statistical Association
anchor_paper.claim: Partial interference gives foundational direct and indirect effects but assumes no cross-group interference.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Boundary/regime-shift pattern, not blocked: the new term is cross-cluster leakage that changes the estimand/null relation, not simply generalizing partial interference.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from partial-interference definitions because cross-group interference just violates the model.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: the question is whether exact partial-interference randomization restrictions can still coincide with ANI contrasts under approximate leakage, and where they stop doing so.

seed_details.one_line: Prior proposal pid_network_exposure_v1 §11 says it does not claim statistical inference, asymptotic coverage, or an efficient estimator. Our seed conjectures that the shared-completion exposure gap induces a second-order variance frontier, because graph-completion compatibility changes the score covariance as well as the identified set.

anchor_paper.author: ThmSmith prior proposal
anchor_paper.claim: The proposal does not claim statistical inference, asymptotic coverage, or an efficient estimator.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Prior-proposal extension pattern, not blocked: the missing object is named by the prior scope flag and is promoted to named estimator covariance maps rather than a generic follow-on.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from pid_network_exposure_v1 because finite identified sets already encode all exposure uncertainty.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: that proposal explicitly stops before asymptotic inference, coverage, and efficiency comparisons.

Our seed conjectures that named network estimator maps have a quotient-row-space phase transition, because ANI, cluster, and randomization scores are different projections of the same spillover response surface.

anchor_paper.claim: identify exactly which estimator or diagnostic each lacks

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: n/a - orthogonal to blocklist: the anchor is a reviewer-identified failure mode and the proposed mechanism is named estimator-map projection geometry.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from the failed panel_honest_did_phase proposal because it already had row-space frontiers.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: that proposal was rejected partly because it lacked named estimator-map comparisons and did not concern network interference estimators.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work BasseFellerToulis2019 would predict that conditioning mechanisms give valid randomization tests under interference. Our seed conjectures that conditioning loses direct-plus-spillover identification unless the orbit sigma-field refines the exposure sigma-field, because validity of a sharp null is weaker than equality of exposure-effect functionals.

anchor_paper.author: Guillaume W. Basse, Avi Feller, Panos Toulis
anchor_paper.claim: Conditional randomization mechanisms yield valid tests under general interference, but the conditioning choice does not automatically identify an ANI exposure effect.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Target-variation pattern, not blocked: the target switch from valid sharp null to equality of exposure-effect functional reveals an identification gap hidden by test validity.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from BasseFellerToulis2019 because their conditioning mechanism already defines valid exposure-contrast tests.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: valid testing under a conditioning event is not the same as equality with a fixed-radius ANI direct-plus-spillover estimand.

proposed_from.literature_map:
Leung 2022 Econometrica: ANI relaxes exact local interference and legitimates fixed-radius exposure IPW plus network HAC, but leaves sharp misspecification and cross-estimator frontiers open.
Leung 2023 Econometrica: cluster robustness is governed by low conductance and spectral clusters, not by exposure-radius correctness.
Basse, Ding, Feller, Toulis 2024 Econometrica: exact permutation tests for group-formation peer effects target design-compatible exposure nulls, not general ANI tails.
Savje 2024 Biometrika: misspecified exposure mappings can define estimable contrasts, creating a gap between estimability and causal interpretation.
Aronow and Samii 2017 Annals of Applied Statistics: exposure mappings make arbitrary interference operational but put hard assumptions into the exposure definition.
Basse, Feller, Toulis 2019 Biometrika: conditioning mechanisms solve randomization-test validity problems, not identification equivalence with exposure-IPW estimands.
Hudgens and Halloran 2008 JASA: partial interference defines direct and indirect effects but rules out cross-group leakage that ANI permits.
Kojevnikov, Marmer, Song 2021 Journal of Econometrics: network HAC depends on decay-versus-density tradeoffs, matching the rho axis of the proposed frontier.
Conley 1999 Journal of Econometrics: spatial HAC is the metric-dependence ancestor for graph-distance bandwidth selection.
The unresolved tension is that exposure-radius correctness, low-conductance clustering, and design-orbit compatibility are different sigma-field restrictions with no published sharp common frontier.

banked_reason: Stage -0.5 ACCEPT@flagship on three consecutive angles (deepest pipeline reach of any run in this batch); all 3 Stage 0 derivations completed; all 3 Stage 0.5 reviews REJECTed on structural novelty — kernel reduces to tautological row-stacking / orbit-decomposition identity (Delta = sup||A_n u||, equivalence iff Delta=0) with substantive econometric content (LLN separation, operator convergence) shifted into assumptions. Each Stage 0.5 reject triggered angle pivot rather than revise. Intervention-judge synthesis fallback PATCH FIRED LIVE on angle 2 (Bug B fix verified). Math sound at field tier for spectral compatibility frontier; flagship kernel structurally untenable — non-equivalence between ANI/network-CR/randomization-test reduces to trivial identity at the population truth.
